inclusion_criteria: Population: Original human studies enrolling healthy adult non-pregnant women from menarche through post-menopause
Exposure/comparison: Studies that compare menstrual cycle phases (e.g., follicular vs luteal) and/or pre- vs post-menopausal status

Outcomes: Report quantitative posterior pole structural parameters (e.g., macular morphology metrics, choroidal thickness, optic disc/retinal nerve fiber layer characteristics) measured by ocular imaging
Study design: Peer-reviewed primary research (e.g., cross-sectional, cohort, case-control) with data enabling between-phase or between-status comparisons
exclusion_criteria: Wrong population: includes males, pregnant or pre-menarche participants, or participants with ocular pathology/systemic diseases affecting ocular structure, or users of exogenous hormones (e.g., oral contraceptives, HRT) unless results are stratified for eligible women
No relevant comparison: does not stratify or compare by menstrual phase or menopausal status
No relevant outcomes: does not report posterior pole structural parameters (macula, choroid, optic disc) with extractable quantitative data
Non-original or non-human evidence: reviews, editorials, protocols, case reports/series, conference abstracts without full data, animal or in vitro studies
screening_criteria.description: Screening criteria to identify original human studies in healthy adult non-pregnant women that compare posterior pole structural parameters across menstrual cycle phases and/or menopausal status.

included_articles.title: Choroidal Morphology and Systemic Circulation Changes During the Menstrual Cycle in Healthy Japanese Women.
Evaluation of the macula, retinal nerve fiber layer and choroid thickness in postmenopausal women and reproductive-age women using spectral-domain optical coherence tomography.
The effect of the menstrual cycle on the optic nerve head analysis of migrainous women.
Effect of the menstrual cycle on the optic nerve head in diabetes: analysis by confocal scanning laser ophthalmoscopy.
Choroidal thickness changes during the menstrual cycle.
Assessment of choroidal vascularity index during the menstrual cycle.
